page.title: Complex Passive and Causative
page.subtitle_html: Complex Passive and Causative - C1 passive reporting, causative have/get, and formal passive patterns.
page.subtitle_text: C1 passive reporting, causative have/get, and formal passive patterns.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Complex Passive and Causative: compact anchors for advanced passive and causative patterns.","rules":[{"label":"Causative have/get","text":"Use have or get + object + past participle when someone arranges for an action to be done.","example":"I had my car repaired at the garage."},{"label":"Complex passive","text":"Use passive forms with reporting, infinitives, and formal processes when the action matters more than the agent.","example":"The policy is expected to be revised."},{"label":"Formal focus","text":"Passive and causative forms often create a neutral or institutional tone in C1 writing.","example":"The documents were reviewed before the decision was announced."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the English Sentence Builder C1 complex passive and causative lesson package.
